mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, compressing discount rates and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL > 3%) signals central bank intervention, leading to higher rates and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in combined growth scenarios are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market (UNRATE < 3%) signals wage pressures and potential rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of low UNRATE instances despite tight labor conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 for any 2-year window, or 20-day returns in rising INDPRO scenarios are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Surging housing starts (HOUST > 1500) signal strong demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite housing growth.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) combined with low rates (FEDFUNDS < 4%) signals stagflation risks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in stagflation scenarios are significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 5%) compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of high FEDFUNDS instances despite tightening conditions.
